basic_info.title: Grandia
basic_info.review: Grandia is a landmark JRPG praised for its innovative battle system, charming characters, and memorable world design. Its blend of real-time combat with strategic elements set new standards for the genre.

play_today.details: The definitive version with official English localization, widely available through PSN and physical copies. Features DualShock support and PocketStation compatibility.
HD Remaster version with enhanced visuals, widescreen support, and multiple language options. Based on PlayStation code but references Saturn for visual details.

recommended_patches.name: HD Remaster Bug Fix Patch
recommended_patches.description: Official patch addressing various bugs including localization errors and game freezing issues (version 1.01.00+)

play_today.details: The original version with superior visual effects, better frame rates in town areas, and building shadows not present in the PS1 port. Requires English translation patch for non-Japanese players.

recommended_patches.name: Sega Saturn English Translation Patch
recommended_patches.description: Applies the official PlayStation English translation to the Japanese Saturn release. Created by TrekkiesUnite118 and team.

optimal_settings: High accuracy mode for best compatibility

description.synopsis: A young boy named Justin inherits a magic stone that leads him on an adventure around the world to uncover the mystery of a long-lost civilization while evading the militaristic Garlyle Forces.
key_features: Innovative real-time battle system with IP bar timing mechanics
Rotational party roster with skill-based character progression
Preemptive strike system based on attack angle and positioning
description.long_description: Grandia, developed by Game Arts over a period of more than two years following their Lunar series work, is considered one of the finest JRPGs of the late 1990s. The game was originally intended for Sega CD but shifted to Saturn during development. Featuring 20,000 frames of animation and music composed by Noriyuki Iwadare (recorded at Skywalker Sound), Grandia introduced an innovative battle system where players must time their attacks based on the IP bar mechanics. The story follows Justin, a young adventurer seeking his missing father while uncovering ancient secrets about the Icarians and the parasitic creature Gaia. The game received near-universal acclaim, with GameSpot calling it 'every bit as worthwhile as Final Fantasy VIII' and IGN ranking it 17th on their top PlayStation games list in 2000.
legacy_and_impact: Influenced battle system design in numerous subsequent JRPGs
Featured prominently in GameSpot's 'Best RPGs of All Time' lists
Voted 73rd greatest game of all time by Famitsu readers in 2006
Established Game Arts as a premier JRPG developer alongside Square and Enix
Continued to receive ports and remasters decades after original release
